findings.evidence: `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/Coalgebra/TensorProduct.lean:69` has `lemma counit_tmul (x : A) (y : B) : ...`; the new proof is exactly `rw [TensorProduct.counit_tmul]`.
findings.fix: Remove this declaration and use `TensorProduct.counit_tmul` directly; `baseChange` is just `K ⊗[k] A`.
findings.issue: `BaseChange.counit_tmul` duplicates the existing tensor-product counit simp lemma.

findings.evidence: `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/Coalgebra/TensorProduct.lean:64` has `lemma comul_tmul (x : A) (y : B) : ...`; the new statement is the same specialized to `K ⊗[k] A` and its proof is `rw [TensorProduct.comul_tmul]`.
findings.fix: Remove this declaration and use `TensorProduct.comul_tmul` directly.

summary: Several new pointwise lemmas are direct specializations of existing Mathlib tensor-product API under the reducible `baseChange` abbreviation.
